outlet_trend_analysis

Bella Oleksy

2024-11-19

Some notes at the top about data processing

Flux estimates– - The numbers don’t match what appears in Heath & Baron 2013 nor other papers like Mast et al 2014 ES&T - For example, NO3 annual yield should be in the realm of 1-4 kg/ha/yr - Here is how I did my calculations:

For each week, I have a solute concentration and a discharge value solute = mg/L discharge = m3/s

[mg/L] * [m3/s] * [1000L/m3] * [86400s/day] *

Missing data:

Missing data are sprinkled throughout, but basically a non-issue. I interpolated between weeks with missing data where necessary but this is extremely rare after 1991. We do not have data past 2023 in the database yet.

Plots

Weekly fluxes 1991-2024

outlet_weekly_flux %>%
  filter(waterYear >= 1991 & waterYear <= 2022) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=waterYear, y=flux_kg_per_ha, color=chem_name))+
  geom_point()+
  geom_line()+
  facet_wrap(~chem_name, scales="free_y")+
  theme_few(base_size = 24)+
  labs(y="kg per ha per week")
## Warning: Removed 474 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).
